successCriteria.description: src/shared/types/leave.types.ts exists and exports a LeaveType string enum with exactly three members: ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY — each with a string value matching its key.
src/shared/types/leave.types.ts exists and exports a LeaveStatus string enum with exactly four members: PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED — each with a string value matching its key.
tests/unit/shared/types/leave.types.test.ts exists with Jest tests that verify every LeaveType and LeaveStatus enum value is defined and has the correct string value.
No files other than src/shared/types/leave.types.ts and tests/unit/shared/types/leave.types.test.ts are created or modified in this phase.

constraints: TypeScript string enums must be used (not const enums, not union types) — the enums must be runtime-accessible for validation and serialization.
The file must use ES module export syntax (export enum) consistent with the project's TypeScript configuration.
No external dependencies may be added — this is a pure TypeScript types file with zero imports.
Jest test file must use the project's test conventions: describe/it blocks, expect assertions, no external test utilities beyond Jest.

ambiguities.description: The intent says "verifies all enum values are defined" without specifying whether tests should only check for existence (toBeDefined) or also verify exact string values. The existing implementation resolves this by testing both.
options: Test only checks toBeDefined() for each enum member
Test checks both toBeDefined() and exact string value equality
Test checks enum key count and member presence
